volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Two Bacchises (Section 5.2.1155a - Section 5.2.1200)

sections.label: Section 5.2.1155a
sections.translation: I really am ashamed to tell you a certain thing .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1156
sections.translation: What is it that you ’ re ashamed about ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1156b
sections.original: Sed amíco homini tibi quód volo credere cértumst .
sections.translation: Still , as you are a person , a friend of mine , I ’ m determined to entrust you with what I could wish . ( Whispers .) I ’ m good for nought .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1157b
sections.original: Istuc iam prídem scio . sed quí nihili es ? id mémora .
sections.translation: I ’ ve known that this long time ; but tell me why you ’ re good for nought ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1158
sections.translation: I ’ ve been terribly touched with birdlime : troth , my heart is pierced by the goad .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1159b
sections.original: Pól tibi multo aequíus est coxendícem .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , ’ twere much better if your flanks were goaded , you worthless fellow .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1160
sections.original: sed quíd istuc est ? etsí iam ego ipsus quid sít probe scire putó me ;
sections.translation: But what is it ? Although I suspect that I myself pretty well know already what it is ; still , I should even like to hear it from yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1161b
sections.translation: Do you see that woman ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1161c
sections.translation: I see her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1161d
sections.translation: She ’ s not an uncomely person .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1162
sections.original: Pol véro ista mala et tú nihili .
sections.translation: Troth , but she is decidedly uncomely ; and you are a good-for-nothing fellow .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1162b
sections.translation: Why more ? I ’ m in love .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1162c
sections.translation: You , in love ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1162d
sections.translation: ’ Pon honor [*] .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1163
sections.original: Tun , hómo putide , amatór istac fieri aétate audes ?
sections.translation: And do you , you rotten creature , presume to become a lover at your time of life ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1163b
sections.translation: Why not ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1164
sections.translation: Because it ’ s a disgrace .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1164b
sections.original: Quid opúst verbis ? meo fílio non sum irátus ,
sections.translation: What need of words ? I am not vexed with my son , nor yet is it right you should be vexed with yours : if they are in love , they do wisely .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1166
sections.translation: ⋯ Follow me this way .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1166b
sections.translation: See , they are moving at last , these allurers and enticers to disgrace . ( To the women .) How now ? Do you this instant restore us our sons and my servant , or am I to try rougher means with you ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1168b
sections.translation: Won ’ t you away with you ? You surely are not a man , to address a pretty woman so rudely in that fashion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1170
sections.original: Senex óptime quantumst ín terra , sine me hóc exorare ábs te ,
sections.translation: Most worthy old gentleman , by whatsoever is upon the earth , let me entreat this of you , that you will cease to attack this error with such great vehemence .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1172
sections.original: Ni abeás , quamquam tu bélla es , malum tíbi magnum dabo iám .
sections.translation: If you don ’ t away with you , although you are so handsome , I ’ ll be doing you some great mischief just now .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1172b
sections.translation: I shall endure it ; I don ’ t apprehend that any blow that you can give , will cause me any pain .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1173b
sections.translation: How smooth of speech she is . O me ! I am in alarm .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1174b
sections.translation: He ’ s more calm already .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1175
sections.original: I hac mécum intro atque ibi , sí quid vis , filiúm concastigáto .
sections.translation: ( Aloud .) Step this way with me in-doors ; and there , if you choose , correct your son .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176
sections.translation: Avaunt from me , abomination !

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176b
sections.translation: Do , my love , let me prevail upon you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1176c
sections.translation: You , prevail upon me ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1177
sections.original: Ego quídem ab hoc certe exórabo .
sections.translation: For sure , I shall prevail , at all events , upon this gentleman .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1177b
sections.original: Immo ego te óro , ut me intro abdúcas .
sections.translation: Yes , I beg of you to show me in-doors .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1178
sections.translation: What a dear man you are .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1178b
sections.original: At scin quo pácto me ad te intro ábducas ?
sections.translation: But do you know on what condition you are to show me in-doors ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1178c
sections.translation: That you are to be with me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1179
sections.translation: You mention all that I desire .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1179a
sections.original: Vidi égo nequam homines , vérum te
sections.translation: I have seen wicked men ; but not one worse than yourself .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1180b
sections.translation: I am as I am .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1181
sections.original: I hac mécum intro , ubi tibi sít lepide victíbus , vino atque unguéntis .
sections.translation: Step this way in-doors with me , where you may be elegantly received with viands , wine , and unguents .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1182
sections.original: Satis , sátis iam vostrist cónvivi : me níl paenitet ut sim ácceptus :
sections.translation: Enough , enough now of your banquets ; it matters not to me how I ’ m received . My son and Chrysalus have choused me out of four hundred Philippeans . If I don ’ t surely this day put him to the torture , may I never receive as large a sum again .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1185
sections.original: Quid tándem , si dimídium auri reddítur , in hac mecum íntro ? atque ut
sections.translation: What , pray , if half the gold is paid you back ? Will you go in-doors here with me , and so control your feelings as to forgive them their faults ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1186b
sections.translation: He ’ ll do it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1186a
sections.original: Minime , nolo . nil móror , sine sic .
sections.translation: Certainly not — I won ’ t — I don ’ t care — let me alone , now . ( Shakes him off .) I had rather punish them both .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1188
sections.original: Etiám tu , homo nihili ? quód di dant boni cáve culpa tua amissis :
sections.translation: Take you care , you good-for-nothing man , that through your own fault you don ’ t lose even that which the favouring Gods offer you . One half of the gold is offered ; take it , and carouse , and enjoy yourself with your partner .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1190
sections.original: Egon úbi filius corrúmpatur meus , íbi potem ?
sections.translation: What , am I to carouse in that very place where my son is being corrupted ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1190b
sections.translation: You must carouse there .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1191
sections.original: Age iam , íd ut ut est , étsi est dédecori , patiár , facere inducam ánimum :
sections.translation: Come then , however that may be , although it is to my disgrace , I ’ ll submit ; I ’ ll induce my feelings to do so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1192
sections.original: egon , cum haéc cum illo accubet , ínspectem ?
sections.translation: Am I to be the witness of it when she is reclining with him at table ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1192b
sections.original: Immo equidém pol tecum accúmbam , te amábo et te amplexábor .
sections.translation: Nay , so far as I ’ m concerned , i ’ faith , I ’ ll recline at table with your own self .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1193
sections.translation: My head does itch so [*] . ( Aside .) I ’ m a ruined man — I can scarce deny her .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1194
sections.original: Non tíbi venit in mentém , amabo , si dúm vivas tibi béne facias
sections.translation: And has it not before this come into your mind , that if , while you live , you enjoy yourself ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.1195
sections.original: tam pól id quidem esse haud perlónginquom , neque , si hóc hodie amissís , post in
sections.translation: that , i ’ faith , is for no very long time ; and that , if you lose the present day , it can never return to you after you are dead ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1196
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1196b
sections.translation: What are you to do ? Do you even ask it ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1196c
sections.translation: I should like , and yet I ’ m afraid .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1196d
sections.translation: What are you afraid of ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.1197
sections.original: Ne obnóxius filio sim ét servo .
sections.translation: Lest I should be exposed before my son and my servant .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1197b
sections.original: Mel méum , amabo , etsi haec fíunt ,
sections.translation: Pray now , my honey ; such things do happen . He ’ s your own son ; whence do you suppose that he is to have money , except that only which you give him yourself ? Let me obtain pardon of you for them both .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1199b
sections.translation: How she does work her way .

sections.label: Section 5.2.1200
sections.original: quod míhi erat , id me exórat ?
sections.translation: She ’ s now prevailing on me against that which I was quite resolved upon .
